The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":7,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":419,"text":"                # Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":584,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-584"},{"index":585,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":6,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicy"},{"index":793,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-793"},{"index":794,"text":"            # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":795,"text":"            # container should be restarted on exit. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":1087,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1087"},{"index":1088,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":8,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":1186,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-1186"},{"index":1187,"text":"                # Path within the volume from which the container's volume","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume","depth":8,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":1188,"text":"                # should be mounted. optional,","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node matches the corresponding matchExpressions; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":6,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.nodeAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-nodeaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1224,"text":"                                                             # listType: atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. optional,","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and adding\n\"weight\" to the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":6,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1336,"text":"                                                             # listType: atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. optional,","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. The node that is\nmost preferred is the one with the greatest sum of weights, i.e.\nfor each node that meets all of the scheduling requirements (resource\nrequest, requiredDuringScheduling anti-affinity expressions, etc.),\ncompute a sum by iterating through the elements of this field and subtracting\n\"weight\" from the sum if the node has pods which matches the corresponding podAffinityTerm; the\nnode(s) with the highest sum are the most preferred.","depth":6,"field":"preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1583,"text":"                                                             # listType: atomic","description":"The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy\nthe anti-affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose\na node that violates one or more of the expressions. optional,","description":"If the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at\nscheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node.\nIf the anti-affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met\nat some point during pod execution (e.g. due to a pod label update), the\nsystem may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.\nWhen there are multiple elements, the lists of nodes corresponding to each\npodAffinityTerm are intersected, i.e. all terms must be satisfied.","depth":6,"field":"requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-requiredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution"},{"index":1708,"text":"                                                            # The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.ephemeralContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-ephemeralcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2293,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2293"},{"index":2294,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.ephemeralContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-ephemeralcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":2759,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2759"},{"index":2760,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":8,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":2870,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2870"},{"index":2871,"text":"                # Path within the volume from which the container's volume","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume","depth":8,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.ephemeralContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-ephemeralcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":2872,"text":"                # should be mounted. Setting false is useful for\nmitigating container breakout vulnerabilities even allowing users to run their\ncontainers as root without actually having root privileges on the host.\nThis field is alpha-level and is only honored by servers that enable the UserNamespacesSupport feature.","depth":4,"field":"hostUsers","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostUsers","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostusers"},{"index":2921,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2921"},{"index":2922,"text":"        # Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod If not specified, the pod's hostname","depth":4,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostname"},{"index":2923,"text":"        # will be set to a system-defined value.","description":"will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":4,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostname","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostname"},{"index":2924,"text":"        # hostname: \"<string>\"","description":"Specifies the hostname of the Pod\nIf not specified, the pod's hostname will be set to a system-defined value.","depth":4,"field":"hostname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostname","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostname"},{"index":2925,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2925"},{"index":2926,"text":"        # HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname","description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname","depth":4,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostnameOverride","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":2927,"text":"        # as perceived by the pod. hostnameOverride: \"<string>\"","description":"HostnameOverride specifies an explicit override for the pod's hostname as perceived by the pod.\nThis field only specifies the pod's hostname and does not affect its DNS records.\nWhen this field is set to a non-empty string:\n- It takes precedence over the values set in `hostname` and `subdomain`.\n- The Pod's hostname will be set to this value.\n- `setHostnameAsFQDN` must be nil or set to false.\n- `hostNetwork` must be set to false.\n\nThis field must be a valid DNS subdomain as defined in RFC 1123 and contain at most 64 characters.\nRequires the HostnameOverride feature gate to be enabled.","depth":4,"field":"hostnameOverride","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.hostnameOverride","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-hostnameoverride"},{"index":2938,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-2938"},{"index":2939,"text":"        # If any\ninit container fails, the pod is considered to have failed and is handled according\nto its restartPolicy. The name for an init container or normal container must be\nunique among all containers.\nInit containers may not have Lifecycle actions, Readiness probes, Liveness probes, or Startup probes.\nThe resourceRequirements of an init container are taken into account during scheduling\nby finding the highest request/limit for each resource type, and then using the max of\nthat value or the sum of the normal containers. The Pod's termination grace period countdown begins before the\nPreStop hook is executed. Regardless of the outcome of the handler, the\ncontainer will eventually terminate within the Pod's termination grace\nperiod (unless delayed by finalizers). Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes\nor until the termination grace period is reached.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks","depth":7,"field":"preStop","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].lifecycle.preStop","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-lifecycle-prestop"},{"index":3234,"text":"                # Minimum value is 1.","depth":7,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3397,"text":"              # spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","description":"spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].livenessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-livenessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3398,"text":"              # terminationGracePeriodSeconds: <int64>","description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].readinessProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-readinessprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3538,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3538"},{"index":3539,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted.","depth":6,"field":"restartPolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicy"},{"index":3608,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3608"},{"index":3609,"text":"            # Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":3610,"text":"            # container should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","depth":6,"field":"restartPolicyRules","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules"},{"index":3620,"text":"              - # Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the","description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the","depth":8,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].restartPolicyRules[].action","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-restartpolicyrules-action"},{"index":3621,"text":"                # requirements are satisfied. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","depth":7,"field":"terminationGracePeriodSeconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds"},{"index":3902,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3902"},{"index":3903,"text":"              # Number of seconds after which the probe times out. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false","depth":6,"field":"stdinOnce","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].stdinOnce","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-stdinonce"},{"index":3923,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-3923"},{"index":3924,"text":"            # Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":3925,"text":"            # termination message will be written is mounted into the","description":"termination message will be written is mounted into the","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepath"},{"index":3926,"text":"            # container's filesystem. recursiveReadOnly: \"<string>\"","description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled.","depth":8,"field":"recursiveReadOnly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly"},{"index":4001,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4001"},{"index":4002,"text":"                # Path within the volume from which the container's volume","description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume","depth":8,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-subpath"},{"index":4003,"text":"                # should be mounted. optional","description":"Specifies the OS of the containers in the pod.\nSome pod and container fields are restricted if this is set.\n\nIf the OS field is set to linux, the following fields must be unset:\n-securityContext.windowsOptions\n\nIf the OS field is set to windows, following fields must be unset:\n- spec.hostPID\n- spec.hostIPC\n- spec.hostUsers\n- spec.resources\n- spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy\n- spec.securityContext.sysctls\n- spec.shareProcessNamespace\n- spec.securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.securityContext.runAsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroupsPolicy\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.procMount\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup","depth":4,"field":"os","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.os","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-os"},{"index":4061,"text":"          # If\nthe RuntimeClass admission controller is enabled, overhead must not be set in Pod create requests.\nThe RuntimeClass admission controller will reject Pod create requests which have the overhead already\nset. If RuntimeClass is configured and selected in the PodSpec, Overhead will be set to the value\ndefined in the corresponding RuntimeClass, otherwise it will remain unset and treated as zero.\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/688-pod-overhead/README.md","depth":4,"field":"overhead","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.overhead","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-overhead"},{"index":4080,"text":"          # <key>: <int-or-string> # intOrString","depth":5,"field":"<key>","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.overhead.<key>","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-overhead-key"},{"index":4081,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4081"},{"index":4082,"text":"        # PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower","description":"PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower","depth":4,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.preemptionPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-preemptionpolicy"},{"index":4083,"text":"        # priority. resourceClaimTemplateName: \"<string>\"","description":"ResourceClaimTemplateName is the name of a ResourceClaimTemplate\nobject in the same namespace as this pod.\n\nThe template will be used to create a new ResourceClaim, which will\nbe bound to this pod. The pod name and resource name, along with a\ngenerated component, will be used to form a unique name for the\nResourceClaim, which will be recorded in pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses.\n\nThis field is immutable and no changes will be made to the\ncorresponding ResourceClaim by the control plane after creating the\nResourceClaim.\n\nExactly one of ResourceClaimName and ResourceClaimTemplateName must\nbe set.","depth":6,"field":"resourceClaimTemplateName","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resourceClaims[].resourceClaimTemplateName","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resourceclaims-resourceclaimtemplatename"},{"index":4149,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4149"},{"index":4150,"text":"        # Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by","description":"Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by","depth":4,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources"},{"index":4151,"text":"        # all containers in the pod. ContainerCreating state. Note that this field cannot be set when","description":"ContainerCreating state. Note that this field cannot be set when","depth":5,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4321,"text":"          # spec.os.name is windows.","description":"spec.os.name is windows.","depth":5,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4322,"text":"          # seLinuxChangePolicy: \"<string>\"","description":"seLinuxChangePolicy defines how the container's SELinux label is applied to all volumes used by the Pod.\nIt has no effect on nodes that do not support SELinux or to volumes does not support SELinux.\nValid values are \"MountOption\" and \"Recursive\".\n\n\"Recursive\" means relabeling of all files on all Pod volumes by the container runtime.\nThis may be slow for large volumes, but allows mixing privileged and unprivileged Pods sharing the same volume on the same node.\n\n\"MountOption\" mounts all eligible Pod volumes with `-o context` mount option.\nThis requires all Pods that share the same volume to use the same SELinux label.\nIt is not possible to share the same volume among privileged and unprivileged Pods.\nEligible volumes are in-tree FibreChannel and iSCSI volumes, and all CSI volumes\nwhose CSI driver announces SELinux support by setting spec.seLinuxMount: true in their\nCSIDriver instance. Other volumes are always re-labelled recursively.\n\"MountOption\" value is allowed only when SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled.\n\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled, \"MountOption\" is used.\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is disabled, \"MountOption\" is used for ReadWriteOncePod volumes\nand \"Recursive\" for all other volumes.\n\nThis field affects only Pods that have SELinux label set, either in PodSecurityContext or in SecurityContext of all containers.\n\nAll Pods that use the same volume should use the same seLinuxChangePolicy, otherwise some pods can get stuck in ContainerCreating state.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","depth":5,"field":"seLinuxChangePolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy"},{"index":4323,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4323"},{"index":4324,"text":"          # The SELinux context to be applied to all containers. It's a required field.","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":4516,"text":"            # Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","description":"Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":4517,"text":"            maxSkew: <int32> # required","description":"MaxSkew describes the degree to which pods may be unevenly distributed.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum permitted difference\nbetween the number of matching pods in the target topology and the global minimum.\nThe global minimum is the minimum number of matching pods in an eligible domain\nor zero if the number of eligible domains is less than MinDomains.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/1:\nIn this case, the global minimum is 1.\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |   P   |\n- if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2;\nscheduling it onto zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2)\nviolate MaxSkew(1).\n- if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any zone.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give higher precedence\nto topologies that satisfy it.\nIt's a required field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","depth":6,"field":"maxSkew","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew"},{"index":4518,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4518"},{"index":4519,"text":"            # TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with this key\nand identical values are considered to be in the same topology.\nWe consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put balanced number\nof pods into each bucket.\nWe define a domain as a particular instance of a topology.\nAlso, we define an eligible domain as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of\nnodeAffinityPolicy and nodeTaintsPolicy.\ne.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\", each Node is a domain of that topology.\nAnd, if TopologyKey is \"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.\nIt's a required field.","depth":6,"field":"topologyKey","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey"},{"index":4530,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4530"},{"index":4531,"text":"            # WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4532,"text":"            # satisfy the spread constraint. - In other","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4544,"text":"            # words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler won't","description":"words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler won't","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4545,"text":"            # make it *more* imbalanced. It's a required field.","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4546,"text":"            whenUnsatisfiable: \"<string>\" # required","description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't satisfy\nthe spread constraint.\n- DoNotSchedule (default) tells the scheduler not to schedule it.\n- ScheduleAnyway tells the scheduler to schedule the pod in any location,\n  but giving higher precedence to topologies that would help reduce the\n  skew.\nA constraint is considered \"Unsatisfiable\" for an incoming pod\nif and only if every possible node assignment for that pod would violate\n\"MaxSkew\" on some topology.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 3/1/1:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n| P P P |   P   |   P   |\nIf WhenUnsatisfiable is set to DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled\nto zone2(zone3) to become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies\nMaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler\nwon't make it *more* imbalanced.\nIt's a required field.","depth":6,"field":"whenUnsatisfiable","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable"},{"index":4547,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4547"},{"index":4548,"text":"            # LabelSelector is used to find matching pods. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are ANDed with labelSelector\nto select the group of existing pods over which spreading will be calculated\nfor the incoming pod. The same key is forbidden to exist in both MatchLabelKeys and LabelSelector.\nMatchLabelKeys cannot be set when LabelSelector isn't set.\nKeys that don't exist in the incoming pod labels will\nbe ignored. A null or empty list means only match against labelSelector.\n\nThis is a beta field and requires the MatchLabelKeysInPodTopologySpread feature gate to be enabled (enabled by default).","depth":6,"field":"matchLabelKeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys"},{"index":4593,"text":"              # - \"<string>\"","depth":8,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys[]","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys"},{"index":4594,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4594"},{"index":4595,"text":"            # MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains. minDomains: <int32>","description":"MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains.\nWhen the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys is less than minDomains,\nPod Topology Spread treats \"global minimum\" as 0, and then the calculation of Skew is performed.\nAnd when the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys equals or greater than minDomains,\nthis value has no effect on scheduling.\nAs a result, when the number of eligible domains is less than minDomains,\nscheduler won't schedule more than maxSkew Pods to those domains.\nIf value is nil, the constraint behaves as if MinDomains is equal to 1.\nValid values are integers greater than 0.\nWhen value is not nil, WhenUnsatisfiable must be DoNotSchedule.\n\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is set to 5 and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/2:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |  P P  |\nThe number of domains is less than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0.\nIn this situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,\nbecause computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any of the three zones,\nit will violate MaxSkew.","depth":6,"field":"minDomains","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","code":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains"},{"index":4616,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-4616"},{"index":4617,"text":"            # nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread","description":"nodeAffinity/nodeSelector when calculating pod topology spread","depth":6,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy"},{"index":4619,"text":"            # skew. Options are: - Honor: only nodes matching","description":"skew. Options are:\n- Honor: only nodes matching nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are included in the calculations.\n- Ignore: nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are ignored. optional","description":"ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\nThe volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\nand deleted when the pod is removed.\n\nUse this if:\na) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\nb) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\n   tracking are needed,\nc) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\nd) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\n   a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\n   information on the connection between this volume type\n   and PersistentVolumeClaim).\n\nUse PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\nAPIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\nof an individual pod.\n\nUse CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\nbe used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\nmore information.\n\nA pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\npersistent volumes at the same time.","depth":6,"field":"ephemeral","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral"},{"index":4981,"text":"              # Required, must not be nil.","description":"Required, must not be nil.","depth":7,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":5003,"text":"              volumeClaimTemplate: # optional","description":"Will be used to create a stand-alone PVC to provision the volume.\nThe pod in which this EphemeralVolumeSource is embedded will be the\nowner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with the\npod.  The name of the PVC will be `<pod name>-<volume name>` where\n`<volume name>` is the name from the `PodSpec.Volumes` array\nentry. Pod validation will reject the pod if the concatenated name\nis not valid for a PVC (for example, too long).\n\nAn existing PVC with that name that is not owned by the pod\nwill *not* be used for the pod to avoid using an unrelated\nvolume by mistake. Starting the pod is then blocked until\nthe unrelated PVC is removed. If such a pre-created PVC is\nmeant to be used by the pod, the PVC has to updated with an\nowner reference to the pod once the pod exists. Normally\nthis should not be necessary, but it may be useful when\nmanually reconstructing a broken cluster.\n\nThis field is read-only and no changes will be made by Kubernetes\nto the PVC after it has been created.\n\nRequired, must not be nil.","depth":7,"field":"volumeClaimTemplate","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate"},{"index":5004,"text":"                # The specification for the PersistentVolumeClaim. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\ncore object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\nWhen this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\nthe specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\nprovisioner.\nThis field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\nif both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\ncompatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\nboth fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\nvalue automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\nWhen namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\ndataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\nThere are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\n* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\n  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\n* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\n  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\n  specified.\n* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\n  in any namespaces.\n(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\n(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.","depth":9,"field":"dataSourceRef","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasourceref"},{"index":5068,"text":"                    # Container creation will fail If the pull fails.\n- Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\n- IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present and the pull fails.\n\nThe volume gets re-resolved if the pod gets deleted and recreated, which means that new remote content will become available on pod recreation.\nA failure to resolve or pull the image during pod startup will block containers from starting and may add significant latency. Failures will be retried using normal volume backoff and will be reported on the pod reason and message.\nThe types of objects that may be mounted by this volume are defined by the container runtime implementation on a host machine and at minimum must include all valid types supported by the container image field.\nThe OCI object gets mounted in a single directory (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.mountPath) by merging the manifest layers in the same way as for container images.\nThe volume will be mounted read-only (ro) and non-executable files (noexec).\nSub path mounts for containers are not supported (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.subpath) before 1.33.\nThe field spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume type.","depth":6,"field":"image","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].image","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-image"},{"index":5376,"text":"              # Policy for pulling OCI objects. Possible values are: Always: the","description":"Policy for pulling OCI objects. Esoteric PEM features such as inter-block\ncomments and block headers are stripped.  Certificates are deduplicated.\nThe ordering of certificates within the file is arbitrary, and Kubelet\nmay change the order over time.","depth":9,"field":"clusterTrustBundle","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle"},{"index":5552,"text":"                    # Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","description":"Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","depth":10,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.path","required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-path"},{"index":5553,"text":"                    path: \"<string>\" # required","description":"Relative path from the volume root to write the bundle.","depth":10,"field":"path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.path","code":true,"required":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-path"},{"index":5554,"text":"","depth":0,"detailId":"line-5554"},{"index":5555,"text":"                    # Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label","description":"Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label","depth":10,"path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector","detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector"},{"index":5556,"text":"                    # selector. Once the signer approves the\nrequest and issues a certificate chain, Kubelet writes the key and\ncertificate chain to the pod filesystem.  The pod does not start until\ncertificates have been issued for each podCertificate projected volume\nsource in its spec.\n\nKubelet will begin trying to rotate the certificate at the time indicated\nby the signer using the PodCertificateRequest.Status.BeginRefreshAt\ntimestamp.\n\nKubelet can write a single file, indicated by the credentialBundlePath\nfield, or separate files, indicated by the keyPath and\ncertificateChainPath fields.\n\nThe credential bundle is a single file in PEM format.  The first PEM\nentry is the private key (in PKCS#8 format), and the remaining PEM\nentries are the certificate chain issued by the signer (typically,\nsigners will return their certificate chain in leaf-to-root order).\n\nPrefer using the credential bundle format, since your application code\ncan read it atomically.  If you use keyPath and certificateChainPath,\nyour application must make two separate file reads. If these coincide\nwith a certificate rotation, it is possible that the private key and leaf\ncertificate you read may not correspond to each other.  Your application\nwill need to check for this condition, and re-read until they are\nconsistent.\n\nThe named signer controls chooses the format of the certificate it\nissues; consult the signer implementation's documentation to learn how to\nuse the certificates it issues.","depth":9,"field":"podCertificate","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate","code":true,"foldable":true,"collapsed":true,"detailId":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate"},{"index":5736,"text":"                    # The maximum allowable\nvalue is 7862400 (91 days).\n\nThe signer implementation is then free to issue a certificate with any\nlifetime *shorter* than MaxExpirationSeconds, but no shorter than 3600\nseconds (1 hour). The requirements are ANDed."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector-matchlabels-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector.matchLabels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-mismatchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.mismatchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. A pod affinity term, associated with the corresponding weight.","metadata":["requiredFields: topologyKey"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector","type":"object","required":false,"description":"A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods.\nIf it's null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector-matchexpressions","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector.matchExpressions","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-labelselector-matchlabels-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.labelSelector.matchLabels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are merged with `labelSelector` as `key in (value)`\nto select the group of existing pods which pods will be taken into consideration\nfor the incoming pod's pod (anti) affinity. Keys that don't exist in the incoming\npod labels will be ignored. The default value is empty.\nThe same key is forbidden to exist in both matchLabelKeys and labelSelector.\nAlso, matchLabelKeys cannot be set when labelSelector isn't set.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-affinity-podantiaffinity-preferredduringschedulingignoredduringexecution-podaffinityterm-mismatchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity.preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution[].podAffinityTerm.mismatchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MismatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select which pods will\nbe taken into consideration. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. Double $$ are reduced\nto a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR_NAME) syntax: i.e. \"$$(VAR_NAME)\" will\nproduce the string literal \"$(VAR_NAME)\". Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless\nof whether the variable exists or not. When this field is not specified,\nthe restart behavior is defined by the Pod's restart policy and the container type.\nAdditionally, setting the RestartPolicy as \"Always\" for the init container will\nhave the following effect:\nthis init container will be continually restarted on\nexit until all regular containers have terminated. Once all regular\ncontainers have completed, all init containers with restartPolicy \"Always\"\nwill be shut down. This lifecycle differs from normal init containers and\nis often referred to as a \"sidecar\" container. Although this init\ncontainer still starts in the init container sequence, it does not wait\nfor the container to complete before proceeding to the next init\ncontainer. Instead, the next init container starts immediately after this\ninit container is started, or after any startupProbe has successfully\ncompleted."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Represents a list of rules to be checked to determine if the\ncontainer should be restarted on exit. The rules are evaluated in\norder. Once a rule matches a container exit condition, the remaining\nrules are ignored. If no rule matches the container exit condition,\nthe Container-level restart policy determines the whether the container\nis restarted or not. Constraints on the rules:\n- At most 20 rules are allowed.\n- Rules can have the same action.\n- Identical rules are not forbidden in validations.\nWhen rules are specified, container MUST set RestartPolicy explicitly\neven it if matches the Pod's RestartPolicy.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-action","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].action","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Specifies the action taken on a container exit if the requirements\nare satisfied. The only possible value is \"Restart\" to restart the\ncontainer."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Represents the exit codes to check on container exits.","metadata":["requiredFields: operator"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-operator","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Represents the relationship between the container exit code(s) and the\nspecified values. Possible values are:\n- In: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is in the\n  set of specified values.\n- NotIn: the requirement is satisfied if the container exit code is\n  not in the set of specified values."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values","type":"array<integer/int32>","required":false,"description":"Specifies the set of values to check for container exit codes.\nAt most 255 elements are allowed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: set"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-restartpolicyrules-exitcodes-values","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].restartPolicyRules[].exitCodes.values[]","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext","type":"object","required":false,"description":"SecurityContext defines the security options the container should be run with.\nIf set, the fields of SecurityContext override the equivalent fields of PodSecurityContext.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-allowprivilegeescalation","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"AllowPrivilegeEscalation controls whether a process can gain more\nprivileges than its parent process. This bool directly controls if\nthe no_new_privs flag will be set on the container process.\nAllowPrivilegeEscalation is true always when the container is:\n1) run as Privileged\n2) has CAP_SYS_ADMIN\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by this container. If set, this profile\noverrides the pod's appArmorProfile.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.\n  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime's default profile.\n  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The capabilities to add/drop when running containers.\nDefaults to the default set of capabilities granted by the container runtime.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities-add","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities.add","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Added represent POSIX capabilities type"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-containers-securitycontext-capabilities-drop","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.containers[].securityContext.capabilities.drop","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"Removed
