often (in seconds) to perform the probe.\nDefault to 10 seconds. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-successthreshold","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.successThreshold","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Minimum consecutive successes for the probe to be considered successful after having failed.\nDefaults to 1. Must be 1 for liveness and startup. Minimum value is 1.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket","type":"object","required":false,"description":"TCPSocket specifies a connection to a TCP port.","metadata":["requiredFields: port"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-host","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.host","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-tcpsocket-port","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.tcpSocket.port","type":"int-or-string","required":true,"description":"Number or name of the port to access on the container.\nNumber must be in the range 1 to 65535.\nName must be an IANA_SVC_NAME.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully upon probe failure.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nIf this value is nil, the pod's terminationGracePeriodSeconds will be used. Otherwise, this\nvalue overrides the value provided by the pod spec.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nThis is a beta field and requires enabling ProbeTerminationGracePeriod feature gate.\nMinimum value is 1. spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds is used if unset.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-startupprobe-timeoutseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].startupProbe.timeoutSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Number of seconds after which the probe times out.\nDefaults to 1 second. Minimum value is 1.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle#container-probes","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-stdin","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].stdin","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a buffer for stdin in the container runtime. If this\nis not set, reads from stdin in the container will always result in EOF.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-stdinonce","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].stdinOnce","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether the container runtime should close the stdin channel after it has been opened by\na single attach. When stdin is true the stdin stream will remain open across multiple attach\nsessions. If stdinOnce is set to true, stdin is opened on container start, is empty until the\nfirst client attaches to stdin, and then remains open and accepts data until the client disconnects,\nat which time stdin is closed and remains closed until the container is restarted. If this\nflag is false, a container processes that reads from stdin will never receive an EOF.\nDefault is false"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Optional: Path at which the file to which the container's termination message\nwill be written is mounted into the container's filesystem.\nMessage written is intended to be brief final status, such as an assertion failure message.\nWill be truncated by the node if greater than 4096 bytes. The total message length across\nall containers will be limited to 12kb.\nDefaults to /dev/termination-log.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-terminationmessagepolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].terminationMessagePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Indicate how the termination message should be populated. File will use the contents of\nterminationMessagePath to populate the container status message on both success and failure.\nFallbackToLogsOnError will use the last chunk of container log output if the termination\nmessage file is empty and the container exited with an error.\nThe log output is limited to 2048 bytes or 80 lines, whichever is smaller.\nDefaults to File.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-tty","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].tty","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Whether this container should allocate a TTY for itself, also requires 'stdin' to be true.\nDefault is false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumedevices","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeDevices","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"volumeDevices is the list of block devices to be used by the container.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: devicePath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumedevices-devicepath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeDevices[].devicePath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"devicePath is the path inside of the container that the device will be mapped to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumedevices-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeDevices[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name must match the name of a persistentVolumeClaim in the pod"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Pod volumes to mount into the container's filesystem.\nCannot be updated.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: mountPath"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-mountpath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].mountPath","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Path within the container at which the volume should be mounted.  Must\nnot contain ':'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-mountpropagation","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].mountPropagation","type":"string","required":false,"description":"mountPropagation determines how mounts are propagated from the host\nto container and the other way around.\nWhen not set, MountPropagationNone is used.\nThis field is beta in 1.10.\nWhen RecursiveReadOnly is set to IfPossible or to Enabled, MountPropagation must be None or unspecified\n(which defaults to None)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"This must match the Name of a Volume."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Mounted read-only if true, read-write otherwise (false or unspecified).\nDefaults to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-recursivereadonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].recursiveReadOnly","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RecursiveReadOnly specifies whether read-only mounts should be handled\nrecursively.\n\nIf ReadOnly is false, this field has no meaning and must be unspecified.\n\nIf ReadOnly is true, and this field is set to Disabled, the mount is not made\nrecursively read-only.  If this field is set to IfPossible, the mount is made\nrecursively read-only, if it is supported by the container runtime.  If this\nfield is set to Enabled, the mount is made recursively read-only if it is\nsupported by the container runtime, otherwise the pod will not be started and\nan error will be generated to indicate the reason.\n\nIf this field is set to IfPossible or Enabled, MountPropagation must be set to\nNone (or be unspecified, which defaults to None).\n\nIf this field is not specified, it is treated as an equivalent of Disabled."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-subpath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nDefaults to \"\" (volume's root)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-volumemounts-subpathexpr","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].volumeMounts[].subPathExpr","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Expanded path within the volume from which the container's volume should be mounted.\nBehaves similarly to SubPath but environment variable references $(VAR_NAME) are expanded using the container's environment.\nDefaults to \"\" (volume's root).\nSubPathExpr and SubPath are mutually exclusive."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-initcontainers-workingdir","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.initContainers[].workingDir","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Container's working directory.\nIf not specified, the container runtime's default will be used, which\nmight be configured in the container image.\nCannot be updated."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-nodename","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.nodeName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"NodeName indicates in which node this pod is scheduled.\nIf empty, this pod is a candidate for scheduling by the scheduler defined in schedulerName.\nOnce this field is set, the kubelet for this node becomes responsible for the lifecycle of this pod.\nThis field should not be used to express a desire for the pod to be scheduled on a specific node.\nhttps://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/assign-pod-node/#nodename"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-nodeselector","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.nodeSelector","type":"object","required":false,"description":"NodeSelector is a selector which must be true for the pod to fit on a node.\nSelector which must match a node's labels for the pod to be scheduled on that node.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-nodeselector-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.nodeSelector.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-os","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.os","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Specifies the OS of the containers in the pod.\nSome pod and container fields are restricted if this is set.\n\nIf the OS field is set to linux, the following fields must be unset:\n-securityContext.windowsOptions\n\nIf the OS field is set to windows, following fields must be unset:\n- spec.hostPID\n- spec.hostIPC\n- spec.hostUsers\n- spec.resources\n- spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy\n- spec.securityContext.sysctls\n- spec.shareProcessNamespace\n- spec.securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.securityContext.runAsGroup\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups\n- spec.securityContext.supplementalGroupsPolicy\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.appArmorProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seLinuxOptions\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.seccompProfile\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.capabilities\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.procMount\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsUser\n- spec.containers[*].securityContext.runAsGroup","metadata":["requiredFields: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-os-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.os.name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name is the name of the operating system. The currently supported values are linux and windows.\nAdditional value may be defined in future and can be one of:\nhttps://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blob/master/config.md#platform-specific-configuration\nClients should expect to handle additional values and treat unrecognized values in this field as os: null"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-overhead","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.overhead","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Overhead represents the resource overhead associated with running a pod for a given RuntimeClass.\nThis field will be autopopulated at admission time by the RuntimeClass admission controller. If\nthe RuntimeClass admission controller is enabled, overhead must not be set in Pod create requests.\nThe RuntimeClass admission controller will reject Pod create requests which have the overhead already\nset. If RuntimeClass is configured and selected in the PodSpec, Overhead will be set to the value\ndefined in the corresponding RuntimeClass, otherwise it will remain unset and treated as zero.\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/688-pod-overhead/README.md"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-overhead-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.overhead.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-preemptionpolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.preemptionPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"PreemptionPolicy is the Policy for preempting pods with lower priority.\nOne of Never, PreemptLowerPriority.\nDefaults to PreemptLowerPriority if unset."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-priority","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.priority","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"The priority value. Various system components use this field to find the\npriority of the pod. When Priority Admission Controller is enabled, it\nprevents users from setting this field. The admission controller populates\nthis field from PriorityClassName.\nThe higher the value, the higher the priority.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-priorityclassname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.priorityClassName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"If specified, indicates the pod's priority. \"system-node-critical\" and\n\"system-cluster-critical\" are two special keywords which indicate the\nhighest priorities with the former being the highest priority. Any other\nname must be defined by creating a PriorityClass object with that name.\nIf not specified, the pod priority will be default or zero if there is no\ndefault."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-readinessgates","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.readinessGates","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"If specified, all readiness gates will be evaluated for pod readiness.\nA pod is ready when all its containers are ready AND\nall conditions specified in the readiness gates have status equal to \"True\"\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-network/580-pod-readiness-gates","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-readinessgates-conditiontype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.readinessGates[].conditionType","type":"string","required":true,"description":"ConditionType refers to a condition in the pod's condition list with matching type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resourceclaims","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resourceClaims","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"ResourceClaims defines which ResourceClaims must be allocated\nand reserved before the Pod is allowed to start. The resources\nwill be made available to those containers which consume them\nby name.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the\nDynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.\n\nThis field is immutable.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resourceclaims-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resourceClaims[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name uniquely identifies this resource claim inside the pod.\nThis must be a DNS_LABEL."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resourceclaims-resourceclaimname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resourceClaims[].resourceClaimName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"ResourceClaimName is the name of a ResourceClaim object in the same\nnamespace as this pod.\n\nExactly one of ResourceClaimName and ResourceClaimTemplateName must\nbe set."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resourceclaims-resourceclaimtemplatename","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resourceClaims[].resourceClaimTemplateName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"ResourceClaimTemplateName is the name of a ResourceClaimTemplate\nobject in the same namespace as this pod.\n\nThe template will be used to create a new ResourceClaim, which will\nbe bound to this pod. When this pod is deleted, the ResourceClaim\nwill also be deleted. The pod name and resource name, along with a\ngenerated component, will be used to form a unique name for the\nResourceClaim, which will be recorded in pod.status.resourceClaimStatuses.\n\nThis field is immutable and no changes will be made to the\ncorresponding ResourceClaim by the control plane after creating the\nResourceClaim.\n\nExactly one of ResourceClaimName and ResourceClaimTemplateName must\nbe set."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Resources is the total amount of CPU and Memory resources required by all\ncontainers in the pod. It supports specifying Requests and Limits for\n\"cpu\", \"memory\" and \"hugepages-\" resource names only. ResourceClaims are not supported.\n\nThis field enables fine-grained control over resource allocation for the\nentire pod, allowing resource sharing among containers in a pod.\n\nThis is an alpha field and requires enabling the PodLevelResources feature\ngate."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-claims","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.claims","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Claims lists the names of resources, defined in spec.resourceClaims,\nthat are used by this container.\n\nThis field depends on the\nDynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.\n\nThis field is immutable. It can only be set for containers.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-claims-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.claims[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name must match the name of one entry in pod.spec.resourceClaims of\nthe Pod where this field is used. It makes that resource available\ninside a container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-claims-request","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.claims[].request","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Request is the name chosen for a request in the referenced claim.\nIf empty, everything from the claim is made available, otherwise\nonly the result of this request."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-limits","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.limits","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Limits describes the maximum amount of compute resources allowed.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-limits-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.limits.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-requests","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.requests","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Requests describes the minimum amount of compute resources required.\nIf Requests is omitted for a container, it defaults to Limits if that is explicitly specified,\notherwise to an implementation-defined value. Requests cannot exceed Limits.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-resources-requests-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.resources.requests.<key>","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-restartpolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.restartPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Restart policy for all containers within the pod.\nOne of Always, OnFailure, Never. In some contexts, only a subset of those values may be permitted.\nDefault to Always.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#restart-policy"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-runtimeclassname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.runtimeClassName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"RuntimeClassName refers to a RuntimeClass object in the node.k8s.io group, which should be used\nto run this pod.  If no RuntimeClass resource matches the named class, the pod will not be run.\nIf unset or empty, the \"legacy\" RuntimeClass will be used, which is an implicit class with an\nempty definition that uses the default runtime handler.\nMore info: https://git.k8s.io/enhancements/keps/sig-node/585-runtime-class"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-schedulername","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.schedulerName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"If specified, the pod will be dispatched by specified scheduler.\nIf not specified, the pod will be dispatched by default scheduler."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-schedulinggates","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.schedulingGates","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"SchedulingGates is an opaque list of values that if specified will block scheduling the pod.\nIf schedulingGates is not empty, the pod will stay in the SchedulingGated state and the\nscheduler will not attempt to schedule the pod.\n\nSchedulingGates can only be set at pod creation time, and be removed only afterwards.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-schedulinggates-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.schedulingGates[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of the scheduling gate.\nEach scheduling gate must have a unique name field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext","type":"object","required":false,"description":"SecurityContext holds pod-level security attributes and common container settings.\nOptional: Defaults to empty.  See type description for default values of each field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-apparmorprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"appArmorProfile is the AppArmor options to use by the containers in this pod.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile loaded on the node that should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust match the loaded name of the profile.\nMust be set if and only if type is \"Localhost\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-apparmorprofile-type","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.appArmorProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of AppArmor profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n  Localhost - a profile pre-loaded on the node.\n  RuntimeDefault - the container runtime's default profile.\n  Unconfined - no AppArmor enforcement."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-fsgroup","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.fsGroup","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"A special supplemental group that applies to all containers in a pod.\nSome volume types allow the Kubelet to change the ownership of that volume\nto be owned by the pod:\n\n1. The owning GID will be the FSGroup\n2. The setgid bit is set (new files created in the volume will be owned by FSGroup)\n3. The permission bits are OR'd with rw-rw----\n\nIf unset, the Kubelet will not modify the ownership and permissions of any volume.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-fsgroupchangepolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsGroupChangePolicy defines behavior of changing ownership and permission of the volume\nbefore being exposed inside Pod. This field will only apply to\nvolume types which support fsGroup based ownership(and permissions).\nIt will have no effect on ephemeral volume types such as: secret, configmaps\nand emptydir.\nValid values are \"OnRootMismatch\" and \"Always\". If not specified, \"Always\" is used.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-runasgroup","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.runAsGroup","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The GID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nUses runtime default if unset.\nMay also be set in SecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence\nfor that container.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-runasnonroot","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Indicates that the container must run as a non-root user.\nIf true, the Kubelet will validate the image at runtime to ensure that it\ndoes not run as UID 0 (root) and fail to start the container if it does.\nIf unset or false, no such validation will be performed.\nMay also be set in SecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-runasuser","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.runAsUser","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"The UID to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in SecurityContext.  If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence\nfor that container.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxchangepolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxChangePolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"seLinuxChangePolicy defines how the container's SELinux label is applied to all volumes used by the Pod.\nIt has no effect on nodes that do not support SELinux or to volumes does not support SELinux.\nValid values are \"MountOption\" and \"Recursive\".\n\n\"Recursive\" means relabeling of all files on all Pod volumes by the container runtime.\nThis may be slow for large volumes, but allows mixing privileged and unprivileged Pods sharing the same volume on the same node.\n\n\"MountOption\" mounts all eligible Pod volumes with `-o context` mount option.\nThis requires all Pods that share the same volume to use the same SELinux label.\nIt is not possible to share the same volume among privileged and unprivileged Pods.\nEligible volumes are in-tree FibreChannel and iSCSI volumes, and all CSI volumes\nwhose CSI driver announces SELinux support by setting spec.seLinuxMount: true in their\nCSIDriver instance. Other volumes are always re-labelled recursively.\n\"MountOption\" value is allowed only when SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled.\n\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is enabled, \"MountOption\" is used.\nIf not specified and SELinuxMount feature gate is disabled, \"MountOption\" is used for ReadWriteOncePod volumes\nand \"Recursive\" for all other volumes.\n\nThis field affects only Pods that have SELinux label set, either in PodSecurityContext or in SecurityContext of all containers.\n\nAll Pods that use the same volume should use the same seLinuxChangePolicy, otherwise some pods can get stuck in ContainerCreating state.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxoptions","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The SELinux context to be applied to all containers.\nIf unspecified, the container runtime will allocate a random SELinux context for each\ncontainer.  May also be set in SecurityContext.  If set in\nboth SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext\ntakes precedence for that container.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-level","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions.level","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Level is SELinux level label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-role","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions.role","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Role is a SELinux role label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-type","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions.type","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Type is a SELinux type label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-selinuxoptions-user","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seLinuxOptions.user","type":"string","required":false,"description":"User is a SELinux user label that applies to the container."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-seccompprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seccompProfile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The seccomp options to use by the containers in this pod.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["requiredFields: type"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-seccompprofile-localhostprofile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seccompProfile.localhostProfile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"localhostProfile indicates a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nThe profile must be preconfigured on the node to work.\nMust be a descending path, relative to the kubelet's configured seccomp profile location.\nMust be set if type is \"Localhost\". Must NOT be set for any other type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-seccompprofile-type","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.seccompProfile.type","type":"string","required":true,"description":"type indicates which kind of seccomp profile will be applied.\nValid options are:\n\nLocalhost - a profile defined in a file on the node should be used.\nRuntimeDefault - the container runtime default profile should be used.\nUnconfined - no profile should be applied."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-supplementalgroups","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups","type":"array<integer/int64>","required":false,"description":"A list of groups applied to the first process run in each container, in\naddition to the container's primary GID and fsGroup (if specified).  If\nthe SupplementalGroupsPolicy feature is enabled, the\nsupplementalGroupsPolicy field determines whether these are in addition\nto or instead of any group memberships defined in the container image.\nIf unspecified, no additional groups are added, though group memberships\ndefined in the container image may still be used, depending on the\nsupplementalGroupsPolicy field.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-supplementalgroups","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.supplementalGroups[]","type":"integer/int64","required":true,"metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-supplementalgroupspolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.supplementalGroupsPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Defines how supplemental groups of the first container processes are calculated.\nValid values are \"Merge\" and \"Strict\". If not specified, \"Merge\" is used.\n(Alpha) Using the field requires the SupplementalGroupsPolicy feature gate to be enabled\nand the container runtime must implement support for this feature.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-sysctls","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.sysctls","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"Sysctls hold a list of namespaced sysctls used for the pod. Pods with unsupported\nsysctls (by the container runtime) might fail to launch.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is windows.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-sysctls-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.sysctls[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Name of a property to set"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-sysctls-value","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.sysctls[].value","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Value of a property to set"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-windowsoptions","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions","type":"object","required":false,"description":"The Windows specific settings applied to all containers.\nIf unspecified, the options within a container's SecurityContext will be used.\nIf set in both SecurityContext and PodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence.\nNote that this field cannot be set when spec.os.name is linux."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspec","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpec","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpec is where the GMSA admission webhook\n(https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/windows-gmsa) inlines the contents of the\nGMSA credential spec named by the GMSACredentialSpecName field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-windowsoptions-gmsacredentialspecname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.gmsaCredentialSpecName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"GMSACredentialSpecName is the name of the GMSA credential spec to use."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-windowsoptions-hostprocess","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.hostProcess","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"HostProcess determines if a container should be run as a 'Host Process' container.\nAll of a Pod's containers must have the same effective HostProcess value\n(it is not allowed to have a mix of HostProcess containers and non-HostProcess containers).\nIn addition, if HostProcess is true then HostNetwork must also be set to true."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-securitycontext-windowsoptions-runasusername","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.securityContext.windowsOptions.runAsUserName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"The UserName in Windows to run the entrypoint of the container process.\nDefaults to the user specified in image metadata if unspecified.\nMay also be set in PodSecurityContext. If set in both SecurityContext and\nPodSecurityContext, the value specified in SecurityContext takes precedence."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-serviceaccount","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.serviceAccount","type":"string","required":false,"description":"DeprecatedServiceAccount is a deprecated alias for ServiceAccountName.\nDeprecated: Use serviceAccountName instead."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-serviceaccountname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.serviceAccountName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"ServiceAccountName is the name of the ServiceAccount to use to run this pod.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service-account/"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-sethostnameasfqdn","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.setHostnameAsFQDN","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"If true the pod's hostname will be configured as the pod's FQDN, rather than the leaf name (the default).\nIn Linux containers, this means setting the FQDN in the hostname field of the kernel (the nodename field of struct utsname).\nIn Windows containers, this means setting the registry value of hostname for the registry key HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\\\SYSTEM\\\\CurrentControlSet\\\\Services\\\\Tcpip\\\\Parameters to FQDN.\nIf a pod does not have FQDN, this has no effect.\nDefault to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-shareprocessnamespace","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.shareProcessNamespace","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"Share a single process namespace between all of the containers in a pod.\nWhen this is set containers will be able to view and signal processes from other containers\nin the same pod, and the first process in each container will not be assigned PID 1.\nHostPID and ShareProcessNamespace cannot both be set.\nOptional: Default to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-subdomain","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.subdomain","type":"string","required":false,"description":"If specified, the fully qualified Pod hostname will be \"<hostname>.<subdomain>.<pod namespace>.svc.<cluster domain>\".\nIf not specified, the pod will not have a domainname at all."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-terminationgraceperiodseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"Optional duration in seconds the pod needs to terminate gracefully. May be decreased in delete request.\nValue must be non-negative integer. The value zero indicates stop immediately via\nthe kill signal (no opportunity to shut down).\nIf this value is nil, the default grace period will be used instead.\nThe grace period is the duration in seconds after the processes running in the pod are sent\na termination signal and the time when the processes are forcibly halted with a kill signal.\nSet this value longer than the expected cleanup time for your process.\nDefaults to 30 seconds.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"If specified, the pod's tolerations.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations-effect","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations[].effect","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\nWhen specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations[].key","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\nIf the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations-operator","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations[].operator","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\nValid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\nExists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\ntolerate all taints of a particular category."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations-tolerationseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations[].tolerationSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"TolerationSeconds represents the period of time the toleration (which must be\nof effect NoExecute, otherwise this field is ignored) tolerates the taint. By default,\nit is not set, which means tolerate the taint forever (do not evict). Zero and\nnegative values will be treated as 0 (evict immediately) by the system.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-tolerations-value","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.tolerations[].value","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\nIf the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"TopologySpreadConstraints describes how a group of pods ought to spread across topology\ndomains. Scheduler will schedule pods in a way which abides by the constraints.\nAll topologySpreadConstraints are ANDed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: topologyKey, whenUnsatisfiable"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector","type":"object","required":false,"description":"LabelSelector is used to find matching pods.\nPods that match this label selector are counted to determine the number of pods\nin their corresponding topology domain.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchexpressions","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchExpressions","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. The requirements are ANDed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchexpressions-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchExpressions[].key","type":"string","required":true,"description":"key is the label key that the selector applies to."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchexpressions-operator","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchExpressions[].operator","type":"string","required":true,"description":"operator represents a key's relationship to a set of values.\nValid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchexpressions-values","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchExpressions[].values","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"values is an array of string values. If the operator is In or NotIn,\nthe values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,\nthe values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic\nmerge patch.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchexpressions-values","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchExpressions[].values[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchlabels","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchLabels","type":"object","required":false,"description":"matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels\nmap is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is \"key\", the\noperator is \"In\", and the values array contains only \"value\". The requirements are ANDed."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-labelselector-matchlabels-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].labelSelector.matchLabels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"MatchLabelKeys is a set of pod label keys to select the pods over which\nspreading will be calculated. The keys are used to lookup values from the\nincoming pod labels, those key-value labels are ANDed with labelSelector\nto select the group of existing pods over which spreading will be calculated\nfor the incoming pod. The same key is forbidden to exist in both MatchLabelKeys and LabelSelector.\nMatchLabelKeys cannot be set when LabelSelector isn't set.\nKeys that don't exist in the incoming pod labels will\nbe ignored. A null or empty list means only match against labelSelector.\n\nThis is a beta field and requires the MatchLabelKeysInPodTopologySpread feature gate to be enabled (enabled by default).","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-matchlabelkeys","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].matchLabelKeys[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-maxskew","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].maxSkew","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"MaxSkew describes the degree to which pods may be unevenly distributed.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=DoNotSchedule`, it is the maximum permitted difference\nbetween the number of matching pods in the target topology and the global minimum.\nThe global minimum is the minimum number of matching pods in an eligible domain\nor zero if the number of eligible domains is less than MinDomains.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/1:\nIn this case, the global minimum is 1.\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |   P   |\n- if MaxSkew is 1, incoming pod can only be scheduled to zone3 to become 2/2/2;\nscheduling it onto zone1(zone2) would make the ActualSkew(3-1) on zone1(zone2)\nviolate MaxSkew(1).\n- if MaxSkew is 2, incoming pod can be scheduled onto any zone.\nWhen `whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway`, it is used to give higher precedence\nto topologies that satisfy it.\nIt's a required field. Default value is 1 and 0 is not allowed.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-mindomains","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].minDomains","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"MinDomains indicates a minimum number of eligible domains.\nWhen the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys is less than minDomains,\nPod Topology Spread treats \"global minimum\" as 0, and then the calculation of Skew is performed.\nAnd when the number of eligible domains with matching topology keys equals or greater than minDomains,\nthis value has no effect on scheduling.\nAs a result, when the number of eligible domains is less than minDomains,\nscheduler won't schedule more than maxSkew Pods to those domains.\nIf value is nil, the constraint behaves as if MinDomains is equal to 1.\nValid values are integers greater than 0.\nWhen value is not nil, WhenUnsatisfiable must be DoNotSchedule.\n\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 2, MinDomains is set to 5 and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 2/2/2:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n|  P P  |  P P  |  P P  |\nThe number of domains is less than 5(MinDomains), so \"global minimum\" is treated as 0.\nIn this situation, new pod with the same labelSelector cannot be scheduled,\nbecause computed skew will be 3(3 - 0) if new Pod is scheduled to any of the three zones,\nit will violate MaxSkew.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodeaffinitypolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeAffinityPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"NodeAffinityPolicy indicates how we will treat Pod's nodeAffinity/nodeSelector\nwhen calculating pod topology spread skew. Options are:\n- Honor: only nodes matching nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are included in the calculations.\n- Ignore: nodeAffinity/nodeSelector are ignored. All nodes are included in the calculations.\n\nIf this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Honor policy."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-nodetaintspolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].nodeTaintsPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"NodeTaintsPolicy indicates how we will treat node taints when calculating\npod topology spread skew. Options are:\n- Honor: nodes without taints, along with tainted nodes for which the incoming pod\nhas a toleration, are included.\n- Ignore: node taints are ignored. All nodes are included.\n\nIf this value is nil, the behavior is equivalent to the Ignore policy."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-topologykey","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].topologyKey","type":"string","required":true,"description":"TopologyKey is the key of node labels. Nodes that have a label with this key\nand identical values are considered to be in the same topology.\nWe consider each <key, value> as a \"bucket\", and try to put balanced number\nof pods into each bucket.\nWe define a domain as a particular instance of a topology.\nAlso, we define an eligible domain as a domain whose nodes meet the requirements of\nnodeAffinityPolicy and nodeTaintsPolicy.\ne.g. If TopologyKey is \"kubernetes.io/hostname\", each Node is a domain of that topology.\nAnd, if TopologyKey is \"topology.kubernetes.io/zone\", each zone is a domain of that topology.\nIt's a required field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-topologyspreadconstraints-whenunsatisfiable","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.topologySpreadConstraints[].whenUnsatisfiable","type":"string","required":true,"description":"WhenUnsatisfiable indicates how to deal with a pod if it doesn't satisfy\nthe spread constraint.\n- DoNotSchedule (default) tells the scheduler not to schedule it.\n- ScheduleAnyway tells the scheduler to schedule the pod in any location,\n  but giving higher precedence to topologies that would help reduce the\n  skew.\nA constraint is considered \"Unsatisfiable\" for an incoming pod\nif and only if every possible node assignment for that pod would violate\n\"MaxSkew\" on some topology.\nFor example, in a 3-zone cluster, MaxSkew is set to 1, and pods with the same\nlabelSelector spread as 3/1/1:\n| zone1 | zone2 | zone3 |\n| P P P |   P   |   P   |\nIf WhenUnsatisfiable is set to DoNotSchedule, incoming pod can only be scheduled\nto zone2(zone3) to become 3/2/1(3/1/2) as ActualSkew(2-1) on zone2(zone3) satisfies\nMaxSkew(1). In other words, the cluster can still be imbalanced, but scheduler\nwon't make it *more* imbalanced.\nIt's a required field."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"List of volumes that can be mounted by containers belonging to the pod.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: map","x-kubernetes-list-map-keys: name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-awselasticblockstore","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].awsElasticBlockStore","type":"object","required":false,"description":"awsElasticBlockStore represents an AWS Disk resource that is attached to a\nkubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\nDeprecated: AWSElasticBlockStore is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree\nawsElasticBlockStore type are redirected to the ebs.csi.aws.com CSI driver.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore","metadata":["requiredFields: volumeID"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-awselasticblockstore-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].awsElasticBlockStore.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\nTip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\nExamples: \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-awselasticblockstore-partition","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].awsElasticBlockStore.partition","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"partition is the partition in the volume that you want to mount.\nIf omitted, the default is to mount by volume name.\nExamples: For volume /dev/sda1, you specify the partition as \"1\".\nSimilarly, the volume partition for /dev/sda is \"0\" (or you can leave the property empty).","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-awselasticblockstore-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].awsElasticBlockStore.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly value true will force the readOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-awselasticblockstore-volumeid","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].awsElasticBlockStore.volumeID","type":"string","required":true,"description":"volumeID is unique ID of the persistent disk resource in AWS (Amazon EBS volume).\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#awselasticblockstore"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk","type":"object","required":false,"description":"azureDisk represents an Azure Data Disk mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.\nDeprecated: AzureDisk is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree azureDisk type\nare redirected to the disk.csi.azure.com CSI driver.","metadata":["requiredFields: diskName, diskURI"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-cachingmode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.cachingMode","type":"string","required":false,"description":"cachingMode is the Host Caching mode: None, Read Only, Read Write."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-diskname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.diskName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"diskName is the Name of the data disk in the blob storage"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-diskuri","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.diskURI","type":"string","required":true,"description":"diskURI is the URI of data disk in the blob storage"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is Filesystem type to mount.\nMust be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.\nEx. \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified.","metadata":["default: \"ext4\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-kind","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.kind","type":"string","required":false,"description":"kind expected values are Shared: multiple blob disks per storage account  Dedicated: single blob disk per storage account  Managed: azure managed data disk (only in managed availability set). defaults to shared"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azuredisk-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureDisk.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.","metadata":["default: false"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azurefile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureFile","type":"object","required":false,"description":"azureFile represents an Azure File Service mount on the host and bind mount to the pod.\nDeprecated: AzureFile is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree azureFile type\nare redirected to the file.csi.azure.com CSI driver.","metadata":["requiredFields: secretName, shareName"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azurefile-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureFile.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azurefile-secretname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureFile.secretName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"secretName is the  name of secret that contains Azure Storage Account Name and Key"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-azurefile-sharename","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].azureFile.shareName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"shareName is the azure share Name"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs","type":"object","required":false,"description":"cephFS represents a Ceph FS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\nDeprecated: CephFS is deprecated and the in-tree cephfs type is no longer supported.","metadata":["requiredFields: monitors"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-monitors","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.monitors","type":"array<string>","required":true,"description":"monitors is Required: Monitors is a collection of Ceph monitors\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-monitors","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.monitors[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.path","type":"string","required":false,"description":"path is Optional: Used as the mounted root, rather than the full Ceph tree, default is /"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly is Optional: Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-secretfile","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.secretFile","type":"string","required":false,"description":"secretFile is Optional: SecretFile is the path to key ring for User, default is /etc/ceph/user.secret\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-secretref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.secretRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"secretRef is Optional: SecretRef is reference to the authentication secret for User, default is empty.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-secretref-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.secretRef.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cephfs-user","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cephfs.user","type":"string","required":false,"description":"user is optional: User is the rados user name, default is admin\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/cephfs/README.md#how-to-use-it"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cinder","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cinder","type":"object","required":false,"description":"cinder represents a cinder volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\nDeprecated: Cinder is deprecated. Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cinder-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cinder.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cinder-secretref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cinder.secretRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"secretRef is optional: points to a secret object containing parameters used to connect\nto OpenStack.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cinder-secretref-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cinder.secretRef.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-cinder-volumeid","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].cinder.volumeID","type":"string","required":true,"description":"volumeID used to identify the volume in cinder.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/mysql-cinder-pd/README.md"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap","type":"object","required":false,"description":"configMap represents a configMap that should populate this volume","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-defaultmode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.defaultMode","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"defaultMode is optional: mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.\nMust be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.\nYAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.\nDefaults to 0644.\nDirectories within the path are not affected by this setting.\nThis might be in conflict with other options that affect the file\nmode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-items","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.items","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"items if unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced\nConfigMap will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the\nkey and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be\nprojected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be\npresent. If a key is specified which is not present in the ConfigMap,\nthe volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be\nrelative and may not contain the '..' path or start with '..'.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-items-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.items[].key","type":"string","required":true,"description":"key is the key to project."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-items-mode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.items[].mode","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.\nMust be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.\nYAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.\nIf not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.\nThis might be in conflict with other options that affect the file\nmode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-items-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.items[].path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.\nMay not be an absolute path.\nMay not contain the path element '..'.\nMay not start with the string '..'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-configmap-optional","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].configMap.optional","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"optional specify whether the ConfigMap or its keys must be defined"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi","type":"object","required":false,"description":"csi (Container Storage Interface) represents ephemeral storage that is handled by certain external CSI drivers.","metadata":["requiredFields: driver"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-driver","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.driver","type":"string","required":true,"description":"driver is the name of the CSI driver that handles this volume.\nConsult with your admin for the correct name as registered in the cluster."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType to mount. Ex. \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\".\nIf not provided, the empty value is passed to the associated CSI driver\nwhich will determine the default filesystem to apply."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-nodepublishsecretref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.nodePublishSecretRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"nodePublishSecretRef is a reference to the secret object containing\nsensitive information to pass to the CSI driver to complete the CSI\nNodePublishVolume and NodeUnpublishVolume calls.\nThis field is optional, and  may be empty if no secret is required. If the\nsecret object contains more than one secret, all secret references are passed.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-nodepublishsecretref-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.nodePublishSecretRef.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly specifies a read-only configuration for the volume.\nDefaults to false (read/write)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-volumeattributes","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.volumeAttributes","type":"object","required":false,"description":"volumeAttributes stores driver-specific properties that are passed to the CSI\ndriver. Consult your driver's documentation for supported values."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-csi-volumeattributes-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].csi.volumeAttributes.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-downwardapi","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].downwardAPI","type":"object","required":false,"description":"downwardAPI represents downward API about the pod that should populate this volume"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-downwardapi-defaultmode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].downwardAPI.defaultMode","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"Optional: mode bits to use on created files by default. decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.\nDefaults to 0644.\nDirectories within the path are not affected by this setting.\nThis might be in conflict with other options that affect the file\nmode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.","metadata":["format:
