represents what type of storage medium should back this directory.\nThe default is \"\" which means to use the node's default medium.\nMust be an empty string (default) or Memory.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-emptydir-sizelimit","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].emptyDir.sizeLimit","type":"int-or-string","required":false,"description":"sizeLimit is the total amount of local storage required for this EmptyDir volume.\nThe size limit is also applicable for memory medium.\nThe maximum usage on memory medium EmptyDir would be the minimum value between\nthe SizeLimit specified here and the sum of memory limits of all containers in a pod.\nThe default is nil which means that the limit is undefined.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#emptydir","metadata":["pattern: ^(\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))(([KMGTPE]i)|[numkMGTPE]|([eE](\\+|-)?(([0-9]+(\\.[0-9]*)?)|(\\.[0-9]+))))?$","x-kubernetes-int-or-string"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral","type":"object","required":false,"description":"ephemeral represents a volume that is handled by a cluster storage driver.\nThe volume's lifecycle is tied to the pod that defines it - it will be created before the pod starts,\nand deleted when the pod is removed.\n\nUse this if:\na) the volume is only needed while the pod runs,\nb) features of normal volumes like restoring from snapshot or capacity\n   tracking are needed,\nc) the storage driver is specified through a storage class, and\nd) the storage driver supports dynamic volume provisioning through\n   a PersistentVolumeClaim (see EphemeralVolumeSource for more\n   information on the connection between this volume type\n   and PersistentVolumeClaim).\n\nUse PersistentVolumeClaim or one of the vendor-specific\nAPIs for volumes that persist for longer than the lifecycle\nof an individual pod.\n\nUse CSI for light-weight local ephemeral volumes if the CSI driver is meant to\nbe used that way - see the documentation of the driver for\nmore information.\n\nA pod can use both types of ephemeral volumes and\npersistent volumes at the same time."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Will be used to create a stand-alone PVC to provision the volume.\nThe pod in which this EphemeralVolumeSource is embedded will be the\nowner of the PVC, i.e. the PVC will be deleted together with the\npod.  The name of the PVC will be `<pod name>-<volume name>` where\n`<volume name>` is the name from the `PodSpec.Volumes` array\nentry. Pod validation will reject the pod if the concatenated name\nis not valid for a PVC (for example, too long).\n\nAn existing PVC with that name that is not owned by the pod\nwill *not* be used for the pod to avoid using an unrelated\nvolume by mistake. Starting the pod is then blocked until\nthe unrelated PVC is removed. If such a pre-created PVC is\nmeant to be used by the pod, the PVC has to updated with an\nowner reference to the pod once the pod exists. Normally\nthis should not be necessary, but it may be useful when\nmanually reconstructing a broken cluster.\n\nThis field is read-only and no changes will be made by Kubernetes\nto the PVC after it has been created.\n\nRequired, must not be nil.","metadata":["requiredFields: spec"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata","type":"object","required":false,"description":"May contain labels and annotations that will be copied into the PVC\nwhen creating it. No other fields are allowed and will be rejected during\nvalidation."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-annotations","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.annotations","type":"object","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-annotations-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.annotations.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-finalizers","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.finalizers","type":"array<string>","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-finalizers","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.finalizers[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-labels","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.labels","type":"object","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-labels-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.labels.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.metadata.name","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-metadata-namespace","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClai referenced"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasourceref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"dataSourceRef specifies the object from which to populate the volume with data, if a non-empty\nvolume is desired. This may be any object from a non-empty API group (non\ncore object) or a PersistentVolumeClaim object.\nWhen this field is specified, volume binding will only succeed if the type of\nthe specified object matches some installed volume populator or dynamic\nprovisioner.\nThis field will replace the functionality of the dataSource field and as such\nif both fields are non-empty, they must have the same value. For backwards\ncompatibility, when namespace isn't specified in dataSourceRef,\nboth fields (dataSource and dataSourceRef) will be set to the same\nvalue automatically if one of them is empty and the other is non-empty.\nWhen namespace is specified in dataSourceRef,\ndataSource isn't set to the same value and must be empty.\nThere are three important differences between dataSource and dataSourceRef:\n* While dataSource only allows two specific types of objects, dataSourceRef\n  allows any non-core object, as well as PersistentVolumeClaim objects.\n* While dataSource ignores disallowed values (dropping them), dataSourceRef\n  preserves all values, and generates an error if a disallowed value is\n  specified.\n* While dataSource only allows local objects, dataSourceRef allows objects\n  in any namespaces.\n(Beta) Using this field requires the AnyVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.\n(Alpha) Using the namespace field of dataSourceRef requires the CrossNamespaceVolumeDataSource feature gate to be enabled.","metadata":["requiredFields: kind, name"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-ephemeral-volumeclaimtemplate-spec-datasourceref-apigroup","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].ephemeral.volumeClaimTemplate.spec.dataSourceRef.apiGroup","type":"string","required":false,"description":"APIGroup Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-lun","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.lun","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"lun is Optional: FC target lun number","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly is Optional: Defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-targetwwns","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.targetWWNs","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"targetWWNs is Optional: FC target worldwide names (WWNs)","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-targetwwns","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.targetWWNs[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-wwids","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.wwids","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"wwids Optional: FC volume world wide identifiers (wwids)\nEither wwids or combination of targetWWNs and lun must be set, but not both simultaneously.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-fc-wwids","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].fc.wwids[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume","type":"object","required":false,"description":"flexVolume represents a generic volume resource that is\nprovisioned/attached using an exec based plugin.\nDeprecated: FlexVolume is deprecated. Consider using a CSIDriver instead.","metadata":["requiredFields: driver"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-driver","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.driver","type":"string","required":true,"description":"driver is the name of the driver to use for this volume."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is the filesystem type to mount.\nMust be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.\nEx. \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". The default filesystem depends on FlexVolume script."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-options","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.options","type":"object","required":false,"description":"options is Optional: this field holds extra command options if any."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-options-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.options.<key>","type":"string","required":false},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly is Optional: defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-secretref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.secretRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"secretRef is Optional: secretRef is reference to the secret object containing\nsensitive information to pass to the plugin scripts. This may be\nempty if no secret object is specified. If the secret object\ncontains more than one secret, all secrets are passed to the plugin\nscripts.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flexvolume-secretref-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flexVolume.secretRef.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flocker","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flocker","type":"object","required":false,"description":"flocker represents a Flocker volume attached to a kubelet's host machine. This depends on the Flocker control service being running.\nDeprecated: Flocker is deprecated and the in-tree flocker type is no longer supported."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flocker-datasetname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flocker.datasetName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"datasetName is Name of the dataset stored as metadata -> name on the dataset for Flocker\nshould be considered as deprecated"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-flocker-datasetuuid","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].flocker.datasetUUID","type":"string","required":false,"description":"datasetUUID is the UUID of the dataset. This is unique identifier of a Flocker dataset"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gcepersistentdisk","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gcePersistentDisk","type":"object","required":false,"description":"gcePersistentDisk represents a GCE Disk resource that is attached to a\nkubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\nDeprecated: GCEPersistentDisk is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree\ngcePersistentDisk type are redirected to the pd.csi.storage.gke.io CSI driver.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk","metadata":["requiredFields: pdName"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gcepersistentdisk-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gcePersistentDisk.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\nTip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\nExamples: \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gcepersistentdisk-partition","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gcePersistentDisk.partition","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"partition is the partition in the volume that you want to mount.\nIf omitted, the default is to mount by volume name.\nExamples: For volume /dev/sda1, you specify the partition as \"1\".\nSimilarly, the volume partition for /dev/sda is \"0\" (or you can leave the property empty).\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gcepersistentdisk-pdname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gcePersistentDisk.pdName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"pdName is unique name of the PD resource in GCE. Used to identify the disk in GCE.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gcepersistentdisk-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gcePersistentDisk.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nDefaults to false.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#gcepersistentdisk"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gitrepo","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gitRepo","type":"object","required":false,"description":"gitRepo represents a git repository at a particular revision.\nDeprecated: GitRepo is deprecated. To provision a container with a git repo, mount an\nEmptyDir into an InitContainer that clones the repo using git, then mount the EmptyDir\ninto the Pod's container.","metadata":["requiredFields: repository"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gitrepo-directory","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.directory","type":"string","required":false,"description":"directory is the target directory name.\nMust not contain or start with '..'.  If '.' is supplied, the volume directory will be the\ngit repository.  Otherwise, if specified, the volume will contain the git repository in\nthe subdirectory with the given name."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gitrepo-repository","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.repository","type":"string","required":true,"description":"repository is the URL"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-gitrepo-revision","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].gitRepo.revision","type":"string","required":false,"description":"revision is the commit hash for the specified revision."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-glusterfs","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].glusterfs","type":"object","required":false,"description":"glusterfs represents a Glusterfs mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime.\nDeprecated: Glusterfs is deprecated and the in-tree glusterfs type is no longer supported.","metadata":["requiredFields: endpoints, path"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-glusterfs-endpoints","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].glusterfs.endpoints","type":"string","required":true,"description":"endpoints is the endpoint name that details Glusterfs topology."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-glusterfs-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].glusterfs.path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path is the Glusterfs volume path.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-glusterfs-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].glusterfs.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly here will force the Glusterfs volume to be mounted with read-only permissions.\nDefaults to false.\nMore info: https://examples.k8s.io/volumes/glusterfs/README.md#create-a-pod"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-hostpath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].hostPath","type":"object","required":false,"description":"hostPath represents a pre-existing file or directory on the host\nmachine that is directly exposed to the container. This is generally\nused for system agents or other privileged things that are allowed\nto see the host machine. Most containers will NOT need this.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath","metadata":["requiredFields: path"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-hostpath-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].hostPath.path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path of the directory on the host.\nIf the path is a symlink, it will follow the link to the real path.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-hostpath-type","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].hostPath.type","type":"string","required":false,"description":"type for HostPath Volume\nDefaults to \"\"\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#hostpath"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-image","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].image","type":"object","required":false,"description":"image represents an OCI object (a container image or artifact) pulled and mounted on the kubelet's host machine.\nThe volume is resolved at pod startup depending on which PullPolicy value is provided:\n\n- Always: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.\n- Never: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\n- IfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present and the pull fails.\n\nThe volume gets re-resolved if the pod gets deleted and recreated, which means that new remote content will become available on pod recreation.\nA failure to resolve or pull the image during pod startup will block containers from starting and may add significant latency. Failures will be retried using normal volume backoff and will be reported on the pod reason and message.\nThe types of objects that may be mounted by this volume are defined by the container runtime implementation on a host machine and at minimum must include all valid types supported by the container image field.\nThe OCI object gets mounted in a single directory (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.mountPath) by merging the manifest layers in the same way as for container images.\nThe volume will be mounted read-only (ro) and non-executable files (noexec).\nSub path mounts for containers are not supported (spec.containers[*].volumeMounts.subpath) before 1.33.\nThe field spec.securityContext.fsGroupChangePolicy has no effect on this volume type."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-image-pullpolicy","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].image.pullPolicy","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Policy for pulling OCI objects. Possible values are:\nAlways: the kubelet always attempts to pull the reference. Container creation will fail If the pull fails.\nNever: the kubelet never pulls the reference and only uses a local image or artifact. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present.\nIfNotPresent: the kubelet pulls if the reference isn't already present on disk. Container creation will fail if the reference isn't present and the pull fails.\nDefaults to Always if :latest tag is specified, or IfNotPresent otherwise."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-image-reference","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].image.reference","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Required: Image or artifact reference to be used.\nBehaves in the same way as pod.spec.containers[*].image.\nPull secrets will be assembled in the same way as for the container image by looking up node credentials, SA image pull secrets, and pod spec image pull secrets.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images\nThis field is optional to allow higher level config management to default or override\ncontainer images in workload controllers like Deployments and StatefulSets."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi","type":"object","required":false,"description":"iscsi represents an ISCSI Disk resource that is attached to a\nkubelet's host machine and then exposed to the pod.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes/#iscsi","metadata":["requiredFields: iqn, lun, targetPortal"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-chapauthdiscovery","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.chapAuthDiscovery","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"chapAuthDiscovery defines whether support iSCSI Discovery CHAP authentication"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-chapauthsession","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.chapAuthSession","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"chapAuthSession defines whether support iSCSI Session CHAP authentication"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\nTip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\nExamples: \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#iscsi"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-initiatorname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.initiatorName","type":"string","required":false,"description":"initiatorName is the custom iSCSI Initiator Name.\nIf initiatorName is specified with iscsiInterface simultaneously, new iSCSI interface\n<target portal>:<volume name> will be created for the connection."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-iqn","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.iqn","type":"string","required":true,"description":"iqn is the target iSCSI Qualified Name."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-iscsiinterface","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.iscsiInterface","type":"string","required":false,"description":"iscsiInterface is the interface Name that uses an iSCSI transport.\nDefaults to 'default' (tcp).","metadata":["default: \"default\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-lun","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.lun","type":"integer/int32","required":true,"description":"lun represents iSCSI Target Lun number.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-portals","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.portals","type":"array<string>","required":false,"description":"portals is the iSCSI Target Portal List. The portal is either an IP or ip_addr:port if the port\nis other than default (typically TCP ports 860 and 3260).","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-portals","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.portals[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly here will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nDefaults to false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-secretref","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.secretRef","type":"object","required":false,"description":"secretRef is the CHAP Secret for iSCSI target and initiator authentication","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-secretref-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.secretRef.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-iscsi-targetportal","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].iscsi.targetPortal","type":"string","required":true,"description":"targetPortal is iSCSI Target Portal. The Portal is either an IP or ip_addr:port if the port\nis other than default (typically TCP ports 860 and 3260)."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].name","type":"string","required":true,"description":"name of the volume.\nMust be a DNS_LABEL and unique within the pod.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-nfs","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].nfs","type":"object","required":false,"description":"nfs represents an NFS mount on the host that shares a pod's lifetime\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs","metadata":["requiredFields: path, server"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-nfs-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].nfs.path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path that is exported by the NFS server.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-nfs-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].nfs.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly here will force the NFS export to be mounted with read-only permissions.\nDefaults to false.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-nfs-server","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].nfs.server","type":"string","required":true,"description":"server is the hostname or IP address of the NFS server.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes#nfs"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-persistentvolumeclaim","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].persistentVolumeClaim","type":"object","required":false,"description":"persistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource represents a reference to a\nPersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims","metadata":["requiredFields: claimName"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-persistentvolumeclaim-claimname","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].persistentVolumeClaim.claimName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"claimName is the name of a PersistentVolumeClaim in the same namespace as the pod using this volume.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/persistent-volumes#persistentvolumeclaims"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-persistentvolumeclaim-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].persistentVolumeClaim.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly Will force the ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts.\nDefault false."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-photonpersistentdisk","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].photonPersistentDisk","type":"object","required":false,"description":"photonPersistentDisk represents a PhotonController persistent disk attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\nDeprecated: PhotonPersistentDisk is deprecated and the in-tree photonPersistentDisk type is no longer supported.","metadata":["requiredFields: pdID"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-photonpersistentdisk-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].photonPersistentDisk.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fsType is the filesystem type to mount.\nMust be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.\nEx. \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-photonpersistentdisk-pdid","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].photonPersistentDisk.pdID","type":"string","required":true,"description":"pdID is the ID that identifies Photon Controller persistent disk"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-portworxvolume","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].portworxVolume","type":"object","required":false,"description":"portworxVolume represents a portworx volume attached and mounted on kubelets host machine.\nDeprecated: PortworxVolume is deprecated. All operations for the in-tree portworxVolume type\nare redirected to the pxd.portworx.com CSI driver when the CSIMigrationPortworx feature-gate\nis on.","metadata":["requiredFields: volumeID"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-portworxvolume-fstype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].portworxVolume.fsType","type":"string","required":false,"description":"fSType represents the filesystem type to mount\nMust be a filesystem type supported by the host operating system.\nEx. \"ext4\", \"xfs\". Implicitly inferred to be \"ext4\" if unspecified."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-portworxvolume-readonly","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].portworxVolume.readOnly","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"readOnly defaults to false (read/write). ReadOnly here will force\nthe ReadOnly setting in VolumeMounts."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-portworxvolume-volumeid","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].portworxVolume.volumeID","type":"string","required":true,"description":"volumeID uniquely identifies a Portworx volume"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected","type":"object","required":false,"description":"projected items for all in one resources secrets, configmaps, and downward API"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-defaultmode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.defaultMode","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"defaultMode are the mode bits used to set permissions on created files by default.\nMust be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.\nYAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.\nDirectories within the path are not affected by this setting.\nThis might be in conflict with other options that affect the file\nmode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"sources is the list of volume projections. Each entry in this list\nhandles one source.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle","type":"object","required":false,"description":"ClusterTrustBundle allows a pod to access the `.spec.trustBundle` field\nof ClusterTrustBundle objects in an auto-updating file.\n\nAlpha, gated by the ClusterTrustBundleProjection feature gate.\n\nClusterTrustBundle objects can either be selected by name, or by the\ncombination of signer name and a label selector.\n\nKubelet performs aggressive normalization of the PEM contents written\ninto the pod filesystem.  Esoteric PEM features such as inter-block\ncomments and block headers are stripped.  Certificates are deduplicated.\nThe ordering of certificates within the file is arbitrary, and Kubelet\nmay change the order over time.","metadata":["requiredFields: path"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Select all ClusterTrustBundles that match this label selector.  Only has\neffect if signerName is set.  Mutually-exclusive with name.  If unset,\ninterpreted as \"match nothing\".  If set but empty, interpreted as \"match\neverything\".","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector-matchexpressions","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"matchExpressions is a list of label selector requirements. If the operator is In or NotIn,\nthe values array must be non-empty. If the operator is Exists or DoesNotExist,\nthe values array must be empty. This array is replaced during a strategic\nmerge patch.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector-matchexpressions-values","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchExpressions[].values[]","type":"string","required":true},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-clustertrustbundle-labelselector-matchlabels","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].clusterTrustBundle.labelSelector.matchLabels","type":"object","required":false,"description":"matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. resource to select"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate","type":"object","required":false,"description":"Projects an auto-rotating credential bundle (private key and certificate\nchain) that the pod can use either as a TLS client or server.\n\nKubelet generates a private key and uses it to send a\nPodCertificateRequest to the named signer.  Once the signer approves the\nrequest and issues a certificate chain, Kubelet writes the key and\ncertificate chain to the pod filesystem.  The pod does not start until\ncertificates have been issued for each podCertificate projected volume\nsource in its spec.\n\nKubelet will begin trying to rotate the certificate at the time indicated\nby the signer using the PodCertificateRequest.Status.BeginRefreshAt\ntimestamp.\n\nKubelet can write a single file, indicated by the credentialBundlePath\nfield, or separate files, indicated by the keyPath and\ncertificateChainPath fields.\n\nThe credential bundle is a single file in PEM format.  The first PEM\nentry is the private key (in PKCS#8 format), and the remaining PEM\nentries are the certificate chain issued by the signer (typically,\nsigners will return their certificate chain in leaf-to-root order).\n\nPrefer using the credential bundle format, since your application code\ncan read it atomically.  If you use keyPath and certificateChainPath,\nyour application must make two separate file reads. If these coincide\nwith a certificate rotation, it is possible that the private key and leaf\ncertificate you read may not correspond to each other.  Your application\nwill need to check for this condition, and re-read until they are\nconsistent.\n\nThe named signer controls chooses the format of the certificate it\nissues; consult the signer implementation's documentation to learn how to\nuse the certificates it issues.","metadata":["requiredFields: keyType, signerName"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-certificatechainpath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.certificateChainPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Write the certificate chain at this path in the projected volume.\n\nMost applications should use credentialBundlePath.  When using keyPath\nand certificateChainPath, your application needs to check that the key\nand leaf certificate are consistent, because it is possible to read the\nfiles mid-rotation."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-credentialbundlepath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.credentialBundlePath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Write the credential bundle at this path in the projected volume.\n\nThe credential bundle is a single file that contains multiple PEM blocks.\nThe first PEM block is a PRIVATE KEY block, containing a PKCS#8 private\nkey.\n\nThe remaining blocks are CERTIFICATE blocks, containing the issued\ncertificate chain from the signer (leaf and any intermediates).\n\nUsing credentialBundlePath lets your Pod's application code make a single\natomic read that retrieves a consistent key and certificate chain.  If you\nproject them to separate files, your application code will need to\nadditionally check that the leaf certificate was issued to the key."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-keypath","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.keyPath","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Write the key at this path in the projected volume.\n\nMost applications should use credentialBundlePath.  When using keyPath\nand certificateChainPath, your application needs to check that the key\nand leaf certificate are consistent, because it is possible to read the\nfiles mid-rotation."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-keytype","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.keyType","type":"string","required":true,"description":"The type of keypair Kubelet will generate for the pod.\n\nValid values are \"RSA3072\", \"RSA4096\", \"ECDSAP256\", \"ECDSAP384\",\n\"ECDSAP521\", and \"ED25519\"."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-maxexpirationseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.maxExpirationSeconds","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"maxExpirationSeconds is the maximum lifetime permitted for the\ncertificate.\n\nKubelet copies this value verbatim into the PodCertificateRequests it\ngenerates for this projection.\n\nIf omitted, kube-apiserver will set it to 86400(24 hours). kube-apiserver\nwill reject values shorter than 3600 (1 hour).  The maximum allowable\nvalue is 7862400 (91 days).\n\nThe signer implementation is then free to issue a certificate with any\nlifetime *shorter* than MaxExpirationSeconds, but no shorter than 3600\nseconds (1 hour).  This constraint is enforced by kube-apiserver.\n`kubernetes.io` signers will never issue certificates with a lifetime\nlonger than 24 hours.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-podcertificate-signername","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].podCertificate.signerName","type":"string","required":true,"description":"Kubelet's generated CSRs will be addressed to this signer."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret","type":"object","required":false,"description":"secret information about the secret data to project","metadata":["x-kubernetes-map-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-items","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.items","type":"array<object>","required":false,"description":"items if unspecified, each key-value pair in the Data field of the referenced\nSecret will be projected into the volume as a file whose name is the\nkey and content is the value. If specified, the listed keys will be\nprojected into the specified paths, and unlisted keys will not be\npresent. If a key is specified which is not present in the Secret,\nthe volume setup will error unless it is marked optional. Paths must be\nrelative and may not contain the '..' path or start with '..'.","metadata":["x-kubernetes-list-type: atomic"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-items-key","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.items[].key","type":"string","required":true,"description":"key is the key to project."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-items-mode","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.items[].mode","type":"integer/int32","required":false,"description":"mode is Optional: mode bits used to set permissions on this file.\nMust be an octal value between 0000 and 0777 or a decimal value between 0 and 511.\nYAML accepts both octal and decimal values, JSON requires decimal values for mode bits.\nIf not specified, the volume defaultMode will be used.\nThis might be in conflict with other options that affect the file\nmode, like fsGroup, and the result can be other mode bits set.","metadata":["format: int32"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-items-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.items[].path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path is the relative path of the file to map the key to.\nMay not be an absolute path.\nMay not contain the path element '..'.\nMay not start with the string '..'."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-name","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.name","type":"string","required":false,"description":"Name of the referent.\nThis field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is\nallowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are\nalmost certainly wrong.\nMore info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names","metadata":["default: \"\""]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-secret-optional","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].secret.optional","type":"boolean","required":false,"description":"optional field specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined"},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-serviceaccounttoken","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].serviceAccountToken","type":"object","required":false,"description":"serviceAccountToken is information about the serviceAccountToken data to project","metadata":["requiredFields: path"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-serviceaccounttoken-audience","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].serviceAccountToken.audience","type":"string","required":false,"description":"audience is the intended audience of the token. The audience defaults to the\nidentifier of the apiserver."},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-serviceaccounttoken-expirationseconds","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].serviceAccountToken.expirationSeconds","type":"integer/int64","required":false,"description":"expirationSeconds is the requested duration of validity of the service\naccount token. As the token approaches expiration, the kubelet volume\nplugin will proactively rotate the service account token. The kubelet will\nstart trying to rotate the token if the token is older than 80 percent of\nits time to live or if the token is older than 24 hours.Defaults to 1 hour\nand must be at least 10 minutes.","metadata":["format: int64"]},{"id":"field-nvidia-com-v1alpha1-spec-job-podtemplatespec-spec-volumes-projected-sources-serviceaccounttoken-path","path":"spec.job.podTemplateSpec.spec.volumes[].projected.sources[].serviceAccountToken.path","type":"string","required":true,"description":"path is the filesystem type of the volume that you want to mount.\nTip: Ensure that the filesystem type is supported by the host operating system.\nExamples: \"ext4\", \"xfs\", \"ntfs\".
